root_text.an8.41:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.an8.41:2.1: “Aṭṭhaṅgasamannāgato, bhikkhave, uposatho upavuttho mahapphalo hoti mahānisaṁso mahājutiko mahāvipphāro.
root_text.an8.41:2.2: Kathaṁ upavuttho ca, bhikkhave, aṭṭhaṅgasamannāgato uposatho mahapphalo hoti mahānisaṁso mahājutiko mahāvipphāro?
root_text.an8.41:2.4: ‘yāvajīvaṁ arahanto pāṇātipātaṁ pahāya pāṇātipātā paṭiviratā nihitadaṇḍā nihitasatthā lajjī dayāpannā, sabbapāṇabhūtahitānukampino viharanti.
root_text.an8.41:2.5: Ahampajja imañca rattiṁ imañca divasaṁ pāṇātipātaṁ pahāya pāṇātipātā paṭivirato nihitadaṇḍo nihitasattho lajjī dayāpanno, sabbapāṇabhūtahitānukampī viharāmi.
root_text.an8.41:2.6: Imināpaṅgena arahataṁ anukaromi, uposatho ca me upavuttho bhavissatī’ti.
root_text.an8.41:3.1: ‘Yāvajīvaṁ arahanto adinnādānaṁ pahāya adinnādānā paṭiviratā dinnādāyī dinnapāṭikaṅkhī, athenena sucibhūtena attanā viharanti.
root_text.an8.41:3.2: Ahampajja imañca rattiṁ imañca divasaṁ adinnādānaṁ pahāya adinnādānā paṭivirato dinnādāyī dinnapāṭikaṅkhī, athenena sucibhūtena attanā viharāmi.
root_text.an8.41:3.3: Imināpaṅgena arahataṁ anukaromi, uposatho ca me upavuttho bhavissatī’ti.
root_text.an8.41:4.1: ‘Yāvajīvaṁ arahanto abrahmacariyaṁ pahāya brahmacārino ārācārino viratā methunā gāmadhammā.
root_text.an8.41:4.2: Ahampajja imañca rattiṁ imañca divasaṁ abrahmacariyaṁ pahāya brahmacārī ārācārī virato methunā gāmadhammā.
root_text.an8.41:4.3: Imināpaṅgena arahataṁ anukaromi, uposatho ca me upavuttho bhavissatī’ti.
root_text.an8.41:5.1: ‘Yāvajīvaṁ arahanto musāvādaṁ pahāya musāvādā paṭiviratā saccavādino saccasandhā thetā paccayikā avisaṁvādako lokassa.
root_text.an8.41:5.2: Ahampajja imañca rattiṁ imañca divasaṁ musāvādaṁ pahāya musāvādā paṭivirato saccavādī saccasandho theto paccayiko avisaṁvādako lokassa.
root_text.an8.41:5.3: Imināpaṅgena arahataṁ anukaromi, uposatho ca me upavuttho bhavissatī’ti.
root_text.an8.41:6.2: Ahampajja imañca rattiṁ imañca divasaṁ surāmerayamajjapamādaṭṭhānaṁ pahāya surāmerayamajjapamādaṭṭhānā paṭivirato.
root_text.an8.41:6.3: Imināpaṅgena arahataṁ anukaromi, uposatho ca me upavuttho bhavissatī’ti.
root_text.an8.41:7.2: Ahampajja imañca rattiṁ imañca divasaṁ ekabhattiko rattūparato virato vikālabhojanā.
root_text.an8.41:7.3: Imināpaṅgena arahataṁ anukaromi, uposatho ca me upavuttho bhavissatī’ti.
root_text.an8.41:8.2: Ahampajja imañca rattiṁ imañca divasaṁ naccagītavāditavisūkadassanamālāgandhavilepanadhāraṇamaṇḍanavibhūsanaṭṭhānaṁ pahāya naccagītavāditavisūkadassanamālāgandhavilepanadhāraṇamaṇḍanavibhūsanaṭṭhānā paṭivirato.
root_text.an8.41:8.3: Imināpaṅgena arahataṁ anukaromi, uposatho ca me upavuttho bhavissatī’ti.
root_text.an8.41:9.1: ‘Yāvajīvaṁ arahanto uccāsayanamahāsayanaṁ pahāya uccāsayanamahāsayanā paṭiviratā nīcaseyyaṁ kappenti—mañcake vā tiṇasanthārake vā.
root_text.an8.41:9.2: Ahampajja imañca rattiṁ imañca divasaṁ uccāsayanamahāsayanaṁ pahāya uccāsayanamahāsayanā paṭivirato nīcaseyyaṁ kappemi—mañcake vā tiṇasanthārake vā.
root_text.an8.41:9.3: Imināpaṅgena arahataṁ anukaromi, uposatho ca me upavuttho bhavissatī’ti.
root_text.an8.41:10.1: Evaṁ upavuttho kho, bhikkhave, aṭṭhaṅgasamannāgato uposatho mahapphalo hoti mahānisaṁso mahājutiko mahāvipphāro”ti.

translation_text.an8.41:0.3: The Sabbath With Eight Factors, In Brief
translation_text.an8.41:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.an8.41:1.3: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.an8.41:2.1: “Mendicants, the observance of the sabbath with its eight factors is very fruitful and beneficial and dazzling and bountiful.
translation_text.an8.41:2.2: And how should it be observed?
translation_text.an8.41:2.3: It’s when a noble disciple reflects:
translation_text.an8.41:2.4: ‘As long as they live, the perfected ones give up killing living creatures, renouncing the rod and the sword. They are scrupulous and kind, and live full of sympathy for all living beings.
translation_text.an8.41:2.5: I, too, for this day and night will give up killing living creatures, renouncing the rod and the sword. I’ll be scrupulous and kind, and live full of sympathy for all living beings.
translation_text.an8.41:2.6: I will observe the sabbath by doing as the perfected ones do in this respect.’
translation_text.an8.41:3.1: ‘As long as they live, the perfected ones give up stealing. They take only what’s given, and expect only what’s given. They keep themselves clean by not thieving.
translation_text.an8.41:3.2: I, too, for this day and night will give up stealing. I’ll take only what’s given, and expect only what’s given. I’ll keep myself clean by not thieving.
translation_text.an8.41:3.3: I will observe the sabbath by doing as the perfected ones do in this respect.’
translation_text.an8.41:4.1: ‘As long as they live, the perfected ones give up unchastity. They are chaste, set apart, avoiding the vulgar act of sex.
translation_text.an8.41:4.2: I, too, for this day and night will give up unchastity. I will be chaste, set apart, avoiding the vulgar act of sex.
translation_text.an8.41:4.3: I will observe the sabbath by doing as the perfected ones do in this respect.’
translation_text.an8.41:5.1: ‘As long as they live, the perfected ones give up lying. They speak the truth and stick to the truth. They’re honest and dependable, and don’t trick the world with their words.
translation_text.an8.41:5.2: I, too, for this day and night will give up lying. I’ll speak the truth and stick to the truth. I’ll be honest and dependable, and won’t trick the world with my words.
translation_text.an8.41:5.3: I will observe the sabbath by doing as the perfected ones do in this respect.’
translation_text.an8.41:6.1: ‘As long as they live, the perfected ones give up beer, wine, and liquor intoxicants.
translation_text.an8.41:6.2: I, too, for this day and night will give up beer, wine, and liquor intoxicants.
translation_text.an8.41:6.3: I will observe the sabbath by doing as the perfected ones do in this respect.’
translation_text.an8.41:7.1: ‘As long as they live, the perfected ones eat in one part of the day, abstaining from eating at night and from food at the wrong time.
translation_text.an8.41:7.2: I, too, for this day and night will eat in one part of the day, abstaining from eating at night and food at the wrong time.
translation_text.an8.41:7.3: I will observe the sabbath by doing as the perfected ones do in this respect.’
translation_text.an8.41:8.1: ‘As long as they live, the perfected ones give up seeing shows of dancing, singing, and music ; and attiring and adorning themselves with garlands, fragrance, and makeup.
translation_text.an8.41:8.2: I, too, for this day and night will give up seeing shows of dancing, singing, and music ; and attiring and adorning myself with garlands, fragrance, and makeup.
translation_text.an8.41:8.3: I will observe the sabbath by doing as the perfected ones do in this respect.’
translation_text.an8.41:9.1: ‘As long as they live, the perfected ones give up high and luxurious beds. They sleep in a low place, either a cot or a straw mat.
translation_text.an8.41:9.2: I, too, for this day and night will give up high and luxurious beds. I’ll sleep in a low place, either a cot or a straw mat.
translation_text.an8.41:9.3: I will observe the sabbath by doing as the perfected ones do in this respect.’
translation_text.an8.41:10.1: The observance of the sabbath with its eight factors in this way is very fruitful and beneficial and dazzling and bountiful.”
